steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main purpose of the text. Map the text: the first sentence states the study's conclusion (thinking about values before discussion makes people more polite under disagreement). The remaining sentences describe the experimental and control groups and the outcome that supports that conclusion.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'widely held belief': The text introduces no pre-existing widely held belief — it presents only the study's own conclusion. ✗
  • Choice B — 'improve the experimental design': The text never critiques or proposes changes to the study's design. ✗
  • Choice C — 'researchers were surprised': No surprise is expressed anywhere in the text. ✗
  • Choice D — 'explain a study's conclusion and how the team arrived at it': The text opens with the conclusion and then details the experimental setup (two groups, different pre-discussion tasks, comparison of civility) that produced it. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
